sources.issuing_organisation: American Diabetes Association Professional Practice Committee
sources.exact_document_title: 6. Glycemic Goals, Hypoglycemia, and Hyperglycemic Crises: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation
sources.population: People with diabetes, with recommendation-specific treatment, age, pregnancy, comorbidity, glycaemic and hypoglycaemia-risk contexts.
sources.clinical_setting: Diabetes glycaemic-status assessment, goal discussion, monitoring, hypoglycaemia review and hyperglycaemic-crisis context.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected A1C or CGM status, individualised-goal discussion, hypoglycaemia history, risk assessment, glucose context, monitoring and clinician-led plan-review documentation. It does not support autonomous interpretation, treatment, medicine doses or UAE service pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 6.1–6.2 — assessment of glycaemic status
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of A1C and/or CGM metrics and an individualised assessment frequency without generating a target or interpretation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 6.3–6.5 — individualised glycaemic goals
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-discussed A1C or CGM goals, health and functional status, comorbidity, treatment risk, burden and shared preferences without automatically applying a numeric goal.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 6.10–6.20 — hypoglycaemia assessment and prevention
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports reviewing hypoglycaemia history at encounters, awareness, high-risk or severe events, clinician-led treatment-plan reevaluation, structured education and glucagon discussion without inserting treatment instructions.

exact_sections.heading: Hypoglycemia definitions and event rates; Table 6.4
exact_sections.locator: Hypoglycemia Definitions and Event Rates; Table 6.4
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines clinically relevant glucose and severe-event context, including events requiring assistance and possible loss of consciousness or seizure, without diagnosing an individual event.

exact_sections.heading: Hypoglycemia risk assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting event frequency, severity, precipitants, symptoms, treatment approach, glucose readings, medication context, awareness and risk factors.

exact_sections.heading: Hypoglycemia prevention; Table 6.7
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports meal, activity, illness, monitoring, driving-safety, education and glucagon context as clinician-reviewed documentation only.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Laboratory Monitoring of Various Chronic Conditions/Diseases – 21
sources.population: People receiving laboratory monitoring for listed chronic conditions, including hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hyperlipidaemia.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for listed chronic-condition laboratory monitoring in Dubai telehealth; partial for broad follow-up and arbitrary laboratory-result workflows.

exact_sections.heading: 1. Background; 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines medication and report verification, DHA telehealth scope, emergency exclusions, and laboratory-monitoring purpose.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Chronic conditions that could be managed virtually
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Includes hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, and hypercholesterolaemia/hyperlipidaemia among the listed chronic conditions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — required laboratory tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists condition-linked baseline and follow-up laboratory monitoring and states that unlisted testing remains clinician-directed.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — statin monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Links statin review to documented ALT and cholesterol or lipid results before clinician prescribing decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — antithyroid and thyroxine therapy monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of TSH, free T4, and T3 over time and notes adherence or symptoms as reasons for earlier testing.
